steps.title: Write the cylinder volume ratio
steps.content: A cylinder with radius $r$ and height $h$ has volume $\pi r^2h$. The second cylinder with radius $R$ and height $H$ has volume $\pi R^2H$. It must be $392$ times as large, so $R^2H=392r^2h$.

steps.title: Factor 392
steps.content: Since $392=49\cdot8=7^2\cdot8$, a radius of $7r$ contributes a volume factor of $49$, and a height of $8h$ contributes a factor of $8$.

steps.title: Check the matching choice
steps.content: Choice C gives $R=7r$ and $H=8h$, so the second volume is $\pi(7r)^2(8h)=392\pi r^2h$. That confirms C.
